CAPITAL MARKETS ARCHITECTURE QUESTIONNAIRE — SCENARIO 2: PARTIAL INFORMATION
=============================================================================

SECTION 1: ENVIRONMENT OVERVIEW
---------------------------------
Application Name: Calypso T360 (Capital Markets Trading Platform)
Environment: Production
Workload Classification: Financial trading
Current Platform: On-premises data centre
Migration Target: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI)
Regulatory: SOX, FINRA compliance required

SECTION 2: APPLICATION TIER
-----------------------------
Framework: Java / Oracle WebLogic
Instances: [NOT PROVIDED — please size appropriately]
Deployment: We need high availability across multiple Availability Domains
Sessions: Several hundred concurrent users
Internet-facing: Yes — traders access the portal from the internet
Internal connectivity: Yes — FastConnect to our main office

SECTION 3: DATABASE LAYER
--------------------------
Engine: Oracle Database (exact edition TBD — we currently use Exadata on-prem)
Topology: [NOT SPECIFIED — we need HA, details unclear]
Backup: Object Storage preferred
RTO: Under 5 minutes acceptable
RPO: Minimal data loss preferred

SECTION 4: STORAGE
------------------
Block Storage: [SIZE UNKNOWN — migrate existing data, roughly ~30-50 TB]
Object Storage: Required for backups
File Storage: [NOT SPECIFIED]

SECTION 5: DATA & ANALYTICS
-----------------------------
Market data: Bloomberg feed required
Monitoring: We need some kind of logging and monitoring, standard OCI stack is fine

SECTION 6: NETWORKING
----------------------
Connectivity: FastConnect — single connection to main office (bandwidth TBD)
Internet: Yes, HTTPS only, WAF required
On-premises: Main office + one remote site
Load Balancing: We need both public and private load balancers
Bastion: Yes, required for admin access

SECTION 7: DISASTER RECOVERY
------------------------------
Strategy: High availability is required — we have been told we need multi-AD
HA Mode: [NOT FULLY SPECIFIED — "regional HA" mentioned by our architect]
Failover: Should be automatic
DR Target: Best effort, standard Oracle recommended practice
